text.title: java-spring Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for java-spring. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Controllers MUST be thin: receive HTTP request, call service, return `ResponseEntity`. Services MUST contain business logic and MUST annotate write methods with `@Transactional` (non-write methods default to `@Transactional(readOnly=true)` at class level). Repositories MUST be `JpaRepository` subinterfaces with custom JPQL queries only when needed; use `Specification` for complex filters. Controllers MUST NEVER return entity types; always return DTOs.
rule.rationale: Layering isolates database schema changes from HTTP API contracts. `@Transactional` on writes ensures multi-step operations succeed-or-rollback atomically. Testability is maximized: slice tests can mock service, unit tests can mock repository. DTOs prevent lazy-loading exceptions and Hibernate proxy leaks.

rule.statement: Entities MUST use constructor injection via `@RequiredArgsConstructor` (Lombok). Entities MUST have an `@Id` field of type `Long` and MUST use `@CreationTimestamp` / `@UpdateTimestamp` for audit columns. Entities MUST NOT be returned from controllers; always map to DTO via MapStruct. Lazy-loaded fields (e.g., `@OneToMany`) MUST be fetched explicitly via `LEFT JOIN FETCH` or converted in the service layer.
rule.rationale: Audit columns track data mutations for compliance and debugging. MapStruct generates efficient mapping code at compile time. Explicit fetch prevents N+1 and LazyInitializationException. Controlling what's serialized prevents information leaks.

rule.statement: Every list endpoint MUST accept `Pageable` as a parameter and MUST return `Page<DTO>`. Queries returning multiple rows MUST use `LEFT JOIN FETCH` or separate queries to prevent N+1. Missing database indexes on frequently queried columns MUST be flagged in code review.
rule.rationale: Pagination prevents scanning multi-million-row tables in a single query, avoiding OOM and timeout. `Pageable` leverages Spring Data's limit/offset and page size defaults. N+1 queries cause latency cascades in prod; `JOIN FETCH` solves it at the repository layer.

rule.statement: Request DTOs MUST use Bean Validation annotations (`@NotBlank`, `@Email`, `@Size`, etc.). Controllers MUST NOT manually check validation; rely on `@Valid` to trigger `MethodArgumentNotValidException`. Services MUST throw domain exceptions (e.g., `ResourceNotFoundException`, `ConflictException`). A global `@RestControllerAdvice` MUST map exceptions to `ResponseEntity` with structured error payloads (status, message, field errors).
rule.rationale: Bean Validation is declarative and reusable. Global advice ensures consistent error format across endpoints. Exceptions propagate intent without explicit try-catch boilerplate. Field errors in the response help clients pinpoint validation failures.
